cards.title: Meta Launches Muse Image Generator
cards.body: Meta introduced a new AI image generator with applications in advertising, decoration, and creator opportunities. The launch has already sparked user concerns about the use of their photos in training.

cards.title: Open Source AI Not Hurting Frontier Labs
cards.body: Open source models and frontier AI labs operate in different phases of the same lifecycle. Success in one area isn't cannibalizing the other.

cards.title: Microsoft Cuts AI Spending with Own Models
cards.body: Microsoft joins other Silicon Valley giants in reducing AI expenditures. The company is shifting to rely more on internally developed models.

cards.title: Discord's AI Moderation Bug Wrongfully Bans Users
cards.body: Discord confirmed an AI moderation issue affected accounts since May, with 200 additional users banned over the weekend. The company has since identified and fixed the problem.

cards.title: Claude Cowork Expands to Mobile and Web
cards.body: Users can now start tasks on desktop and receive updates on mobile without keeping their laptop open. The update enables seamless cross-device workflow continuity.

cards.title: Savi Launches App to Prevent AI Scams
cards.body: The startup raised $7 million in seed funding to combat realistic AI-generated scams like kidnapping ransom demands. The app launches Tuesday for iPhone and Android.

cards.title: Autonomous Vehicles Fight in Ukraine Conflict
cards.body: Forterra has deployed over 100 self-driving ATVs in Ukrainian combat zones. The deployment marks a significant real-world application of autonomous ground vehicles.

cards.title: AI Ransomware Attack Required Human Involvement
cards.body: An AI agent executed a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but humans still selected the victim and set up infrastructure. The attack wasn't fully autonomous despite automation of technical execution.

cards.title: SK Hynix IPO Expected Friday
cards.body: The memory maker is benefiting from the AI boom and preparing for a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO. US investors will gain access to the company riding the AI infrastructure wave.

cards.title: Vercel CEO on Models vs. Agents Split
cards.body: Guillermo Rauch discusses optimizing production deployment through price/performance analysis. The conversation centers on separating model infrastructure from AI agent implementations.

cards.title: iOS 27 Beta Customizes Siri's Voice
cards.body: Users can adjust Siri's pace and expressivity in the latest beta release. Apple continues rebuilding its assistant around generative AI technology.

cards.title: Tech Layoffs in 2026 Name AI Factor
cards.body: Major companies have announced significant layoffs citing AI as a stated reason. The trend reflects workforce restructuring across the industry.

cards.title: Google Stores More Data to Train AI
cards.body: Google's privacy settings now allow storage of images, files, and audio/video to improve AI models. Users can opt out of this data collection.

cards.title: Microsoft Lays Off 4,800 Employees
cards.body: Microsoft cut 2.1% of its global workforce, hitting Xbox and commercial sales hardest. The layoffs are part of ongoing AI-related workforce restructuring.

cards.title: Reddit Uses LLMs to Combat LLM Spam
cards.body: Reddit deployed language models to fight spam largely created by AI systems. Platforms must use AI to combat AI-generated content problems.

cards.title: Station F Launches AI Accelerator Program
cards.body: The Paris-based startup hub founded by Xavier Niel is gearing up for F/ai accelerator. The program aims to position Station F as Europe's AI startup launchpad.

cards.title: Amazon Stops Accepting Mechanical Turk Customers
cards.body: Amazon's Mechanical Turk crowdsourcing platform will cease accepting new customers. The move marks the end of an era for the human labeling service.

cards.title: Google Commercial Features AI-Assisted Declaration
cards.body: A new Google Workspace commercial imagines the Founding Fathers using AI tools. The ad reflects 250 years of hypothetical technological advancement.

cards.title: Midjourney Demands Studios Reveal AI Usage
cards.body: Midjourney is seeking to compel three Hollywood studios to disclose their AI usage. The demand is part of an ongoing legal dispute.

cards.title: Alibaba Bans Claude Code as High-Risk
cards.body: Alibaba classified Claude Code as high-risk software and banned employees from using it. The move reflects concerns about AI tool security.

cards.title: Meta's Muse Image Generator Sparks Photo Rights Backlash
cards.body: Meta launched Muse Image, a new AI generator with applications in advertising and creative content. Users are pushing back over concerns about the use of their photos in training the model.

cards.title: Open Source AI Isn't Cannibalizing Frontier Labs Yet
cards.body: Open source AI models and frontier labs operate in different phases of the same lifecycle rather than competing directly. This coexistence is benefiting both segments of the AI ecosystem.

cards.title: Microsoft Cuts AI Spending, Pivots to Internal Models
cards.body: Microsoft joins other Silicon Valley giants in reducing AI expenditures. The company is increasingly relying on its own models instead of expensive third-party solutions.

cards.title: Discord's AI Moderation Bug Wrongfully Bans Users
cards.body: Discord confirmed a critical AI moderation error that falsely banned users over harmless images starting in May. The issue affected over 200 accounts before the team identified and fixed the problem.

cards.title: Claude Cowork Expands to Mobile and Web Platforms
cards.body: Users can now start tasks on desktop and continue across mobile devices even with their laptop closed. This cross-platform expansion makes Claude's AI assistant more accessible throughout the workday.

cards.title: Savi Protects Consumers From Realistic AI Scams
cards.body: The startup raised $7 million in seed funding to combat AI-generated kidnapping ransom scams and voice cloning fraud. Savi's app launches Tuesday on iPhone and Android.

cards.title: AI-Assisted Ransomware Attack Still Needed Humans
cards.body: An AI agent executed a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but humans still chose the victim and set up infrastructure. The attack wasn't fully autonomous cybercrime.

cards.title: Apple Customizes Siri With Generative AI Improvements
cards.body: iOS 27 beta lets users adjust Siri's pace and expressivity as Apple rebuilds the assistant around generative AI. The update aims to make Siri feel more natural and personalized.

cards.title: Tech Giants Use AI as Justification for Layoffs
cards.body: Major tech companies have announced significant workforce cuts in 2026 citing AI as a stated factor. The layoffs reflect broader concerns about AI replacing traditional jobs.

cards.title: Google Stores Your Data to Improve AI Models
cards.body: Google's recent privacy policy change allows the company to store more user data including images, files, and audio for AI training. Users can opt out of this data collection.

cards.title: Microsoft Cuts Nearly 5,000 Jobs Amid AI Shift
cards.body: Microsoft laid off 4,800 employees (2.1% of workforce), with Xbox and commercial sales hit hardest. The cuts reflect ongoing fears about AI replacing human workers.

cards.title: Reddit Uses LLMs to Combat LLM-Generated Spam
cards.body: Reddit is leveraging large language models to solve spam problems that AI systems partly created. Platforms are increasingly fighting fire with fire in the AI era.

cards.title: Station F Launches Europe's AI Startup Accelerator
cards.body: Paris-based Station F is ramping up its F/ai accelerator program for European AI startups. The initiative positions the hub as a key launchpad for promising AI ventures.

cards.title: Midjourney Demands Hollywood Studios Reveal AI Usage
cards.body: Midjourney is seeking to compel three Hollywood studios to disclose how they use AI internally. The legal dispute aims to establish transparency in studio AI practices.

cards.title: Alibaba Bans Employees From Using Claude Code
cards.body: Alibaba has classified Claude Code as high-risk software and restricted employee access. The move reflects corporate concerns about proprietary AI tool security.

cards.title: Sam Altman's AI Wealth Sharing Proposal Resurfaces
cards.body: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's plan for Americans to share in AI-generated wealth is back in the spotlight. The proposal suggests average families could have a $300 stake in OpenAI.

cards.title: Organizations Scale AI Infrastructure for Agentic Systems
cards.body: IT leaders are expanding AI use cases as capabilities advance toward autonomous agents. The rapid evolution introduces new risks that require thoughtful infrastructure investment.

cards.title: Achieving Operational Excellence With AI Technologies
cards.body: AI is transforming how organizations implement frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management. The technology brings new clarity to complex operational challenges.

cards.title: Data Centers' Energy Demand Threatens Made in America
cards.body: AI data center expansion is driving massive energy consumption that threatens infrastructure plans. The power demands pose challenges for domestic manufacturing goals.

cards.title: China's DeepSeek Plans Chip Independence From US
cards.body: Facing US export controls, DeepSeek is developing its own semiconductor chips. The move signals China's push for self-reliance in AI hardware manufacturing.

cards.title: AI Could Enable Autonomous Robot Workers in Homes
cards.body: Advanced AI is making autonomous robot workers feasible for both workplace and residential environments. The technology promises to transform labor across multiple sectors.

cards.title: Secret Claude Tracker Contradicts Anthropic's Privacy Stance
cards.body: A hidden tracker discovered in Claude shocked users given Anthropic's anti-surveillance positioning. The revelation raises questions about corporate AI privacy practices.
